steps.title: Find the demand slope
steps.content:
Use the two given price-demand points, $(40,20{,}000)$ and $(60,15{,}000)$:
$$
\frac{15{,}000-20{,}000}{60-40}=-250
$$
Demand decreases by $250$ units for each dollar increase in price.

steps.title: Move from the known price
steps.content:
A price of $55$ dollars is $15$ dollars above $40$ dollars, so the demand decreases by
$$
250(15)=3{,}750
$$

steps.title: Subtract the decrease
steps.content: Subtract that decrease from the demand at $40$ dollars: $$ 20{,}000-3{,}750=16{,}250 $$ The answer is A.
